author: maintainer-agent

patch.value:
## Rationale
25k+ history entries exist because item mutation paths call the history writer — but nothing proves coverage is complete. A new mutating command (or SDK primitive) that forgets to record history would silently break the full-proof guarantee that any item can be restored to any version. The contracts table already classifies mutating actions (register-mutation.ts wiring), so the enumeration is free and the gate cannot drift from the surface.

## Phasing
1. Item-scope mutations now: create/update/close/delete/append/claim/release/copy/restore/comments/notes/learnings/files/docs/deps/test-link/plan mutations/update-many/close-many/history-redact/repair/compact (audited rewrites must leave their audit marker).
2. Workspace-scope after pm-klo8: config/schema/profile/init/extension-state.

## Notes
- Assert via the SDK read path (history list) not raw file reads where possible, to double as an SDK-first exercise (pm-usfg).
- Explicit known-unaudited allowlist mirrors the eslint-suppressions ratchet pattern: shrinking-only.
History completeness gate: contract-driven test proving every mutating CLI/SDK action appends a history entry

The append-only-history invariant (all mutations leave permanent JSONL proof) is currently enforced by convention only; add a contract-enumerated gate so no mutating command can ship without recording history.

A test enumerates every mutating action from the contracts/register-mutation tables (single source of truth, no hand-maintained list), executes each against a sandbox workspace, and asserts a history entry is appended with correct op and author; item-scope mutations gate immediately; workspace-scope mutations gate once pm-klo8 lands (until then the test asserts the known-unaudited list explicitly so any new unaudited mutation still fails); adding a mutating command without history coverage fails CI; the invariant is documented in the history docs.

message: Filed from 2026-07-07 all-status backlog review: invariant needs a gate

author: maintainer-agent
message: Phase-2 scope arrives with the workspace stream

value.author: codex-root
value.text: History-completeness audit 2026-07-13: before this audit mutated the tracker, all 1,792 discovered history streams and all 28,195 events were read; integrity checks found zero missing streams, unreadable logs, hash mismatches, chain mismatches, or history drift. Item mutation integrity is therefore currently healthy. Keep this item open because workspace-scoped configuration, schema, profile, initialization, and other non-item mutation paths still need explicit immutable audit coverage and restoration semantics, coordinated with pm-klo8.

author: claude-code-agent
message: Start broad SDK/CLI mutation-integrity implementation: provenance enforcement, history completeness, secret guard, and stale-WIP governance share one acceptance surface.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Investigation evidence: the current repository has multiple overlapping mutation inventories (CLI registration families, migration write gates, telemetry roots, MCP action contracts). A truthful contract-driven execute-every-mutation gate requires first consolidating those sources into one executable registration contract; this PR does not pretend a static scope list satisfies that acceptance criterion. Returning this item to open while the three fully implemented mutation-integrity items remain active.

author: claude-code-agent
message: Return history completeness gate to open; acceptance requires canonical executable mutation contracts beyond this PR's implemented guardrails
